title: Historical Patient Identity Cannot Be Reconstructed
scenario: A historical clinical artifact exists, but the system cannot reconstruct which patient identity was bound to the artifact at authorization time.
initial_state: The artifact was recorded as part of a prior clinical workflow.
state_transition: Required identity linkage evidence is missing during execution evaluation.
execution_boundary: The clinical action is evaluated before relying on the historical artifact.
constitutional_evaluation: Artifact existence does not establish patient identity continuity unless the identity binding can be reconstructed.
constitutional_basis: Execution is inadmissible because the historical artifact cannot be tied to a continuous patient identity.
proof_conclusion: Clinical execution must fail closed when patient identity continuity cannot be reconstructed from available evidence.
clinical_action: Execute clinical action based on historical artifact
